parsed.artifact_title: Determination: Event Identity Ownership During Continuation

claims.text: Concrete event identity is scheduler-owned and is not accepted from model-authored continuation text.
Run names are human-readable labels and are not treated as durable scheduler identifiers.
Record uniquifiers are bound by the framework after the producing wake completes.

parsed.conclusion: The scheduler owns concrete event identity during continuation. The model is not permitted to author or assert event identity in continuation text. Run names are human-readable labels only and carry no durable identifier semantics. Record uniquifiers are assigned by the framework after the producing wake completes, not during model-authored continuation.
declared_losses: The bounded corpus does not specify scheduler error-handling behavior when a model attempts to assert event identity in continuation text.
parsed.recommended_action: Continuation handlers must reject any model-authored event identity claims. Identity assignment, uniquifier generation, and durable naming remain scheduler-owned operations performed outside of model continuation scope.

content.text: Concrete event identity is scheduler-owned and is not accepted from model-authored continuation text.
Run names are human-readable labels and are not treated as durable scheduler identifiers.
Record uniquifiers are bound by the framework after the producing wake completes.
Unrelated UI note: avoid oversized rounded purple capsules.

request_payload.instruction: Write the final artifact. Cite only evidence records that support claims. Do not cite distractors. If a limitation remains, include it in declared_losses.
request_payload.task: Write a determination note about whether the scheduler or the model owns event identity during continuation.
